1018	\begin{lemma}[Pushout cancellation of base change (natively \(R'\)-linear)]
1019	  \label{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1020	  \lean{Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange}
1021	  \mathlibok
1022	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1023	  Let \(R \to S\) and \(R \to A\) be commutative-ring maps and let \(B\) be an
1024	  \(A\)-algebra and \(S\)-algebra making the square \(R \to S,\ R \to A,\ A \to B,\
1025	  S \to B\) a pushout of commutative rings, i.e.\ \([\operatorname{Algebra.IsPushout} R S
1026	  A B]\). Then for any \(A\)-module \(M\) there is a bundled \(S\)-linear equivalence
1027	  \[
1028	    \operatorname{cancelBaseChange} R\,S\,A\,B\,M :
1029	    B \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; S \otimes_R M ,
1030	  \]
1031	  with \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) and its inverse computed on elementary tensors
1032	  by \texttt{cancelBaseChange\_tmul} / \texttt{cancelBaseChange\_symm\_tmul}. The
1033	  \(S\)-linearity is part of the bundled \(\operatorname{LinearEquiv}\): there is
1034	  \emph{no} separate \texttt{map\_smul'} obligation to discharge. Instantiated at
1035	  \(S = R'\), \(B = A \otimes_R R'\) — for which the pushout instance
1036	  \(\operatorname{Algebra.IsPushout} R\,R'\,A\,(A \otimes_R R')\) is supplied for free by
1037	  \(\operatorname{TensorProduct.isPushout'}\) — it is the natively \(R'\)-linear
1038	  cancellation \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \cong R' \otimes_R M\) used as the core of
1039	  the regrouping equivalence below.

1239	% These helpers establish that pullback along a scheme isomorphism is an equivalence
1240	% of module categories; the adjunction unit of an equivalence is an isomorphism,
1241	% used in the codomain-read computation.

1243	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1244	[Pullback along an isomorphism is an equivalence of module categories]
1245	  \label{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1246	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1247	  Let \(f : X \to Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes. Then the pullback functor on
1248	  \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is
1249	  one half of an equivalence of categories
1250	  \(Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \simeq X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\), with quasi-inverse
1251	  \((f^{-1})^*\).
1252	\end{lemma}
1253	\begin{proof}
1254	
1255	  Assemble the equivalence from the functor \(f^*\) and the candidate inverse
1256	  \((f^{-1})^*\). The unit and counit isomorphisms are built from the pseudofunctor
1257	  coherences of pullback: \((f^{-1})^* \circ f^* \cong (f \circ f^{-1})^* = \mathrm{id}^*
1258	  \cong \mathrm{id}\) and symmetrically, using
1259	  \(f \circ f^{-1} = \mathrm{id}_Y\), \(f^{-1} \circ f = \mathrm{id}_X\)
1260	  (composition-of-pullbacks, congruence under equal maps, and pullback-of-identity).
1261	  The triangle identities are forced by these coherences.
1262	\end{proof}
1263	
1264	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1265	[The pullback functor along an isomorphism is an equivalence]
1266	  \label{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
1267	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
1268	  \uses{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1269	  Consequently, for an isomorphism \(f : X \to Y\) the functor
1270	  \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is an equivalence of
1271	  categories. In particular its adjunction unit is a natural isomorphism, so
1272	  \((\eta_{f})_{W}\) is invertible on every object \(W\).
1273	\end{lemma}
1274	\begin{proof}
1275	
1276	  The functor underlying the equivalence of
1277	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso} is exactly \(f^*\), so \(f^*\) is an
1278	  equivalence; an equivalence's unit is a natural isomorphism.
1279	\end{proof}

1285	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1286	[Regrouping isomorphism for the section-level mate]
1287	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1288	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1289	  % NOTE: tensor-order convention. The Lean uses Mathlib's `pullbackSpecIso`
1290	  % orientation `A ⊗[R] R'` (so the codomain is `(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` and the generator
1291	  % goes `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`); the prose below writes `R' ⊗_R A`. The two are the
1292	  % same module up to `TensorProduct.comm` — a faithful re-orientation, no content change.
1293	  \uses{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1294	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1295	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
1296	  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
1297	  % L933--938).
1298	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
1299	  % equality
1300	  % $$
1301	  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
1302	  % $$
1303	  % as $R'$-modules."
1304	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
1305	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
1306	  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\) and \(\varphi : R \to A\) be ring homomorphisms and \(M\) an
1307	  \(A\)-module. There is a bundled \(R'\)-linear isomorphism
1308	  \[
1309	    \operatorname{regroup} :
1310	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M ,
1311	  \]
1312	  the ``equality as \(R'\)-modules'' of the source, sending the generator
1313	  \((r' \otimes 1) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes m\); equivalently its inverse sends
1314	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\). It carries \emph{no} flatness
1315	  hypothesis.
1316	\end{lemma}
1317	
1318	\begin{proof}
1319	  \uses{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1320	  The underlying \(R'\)-linear equivalence is supplied, already as a bundled
1321	  \(\operatorname{LinearEquiv}\) over \(R'\), by the natively \(R'\)-linear
1322	  pushout-cancellation equivalence of
1323	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}. Crucially, that helper bundles
1324	  \(R'\)-linearity \emph{internally}: there is no hand-written scalar-compatibility
1325	  obligation, no induction over elementary tensors, and in particular \emph{no} residual
1326	  \(r' \cdot 0 = 0\) bookkeeping branch. The only work left to this proof is therefore to
1327	  promote that \(R'\)-linear equivalence to a morphism of \(R'\)-module \emph{objects} in
1328	  the exact extension/restriction-of-scalars packaging that the codomain and domain reads
1329	  produce. We work in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation
1330	  (the same module as \(R' \otimes_R A\) up to the canonical commutativity isomorphism,
1331	  per the tensor-order convention note).
1332	
1333	  \emph{The lone residual obstruction is the \(A\)-action diamond, resolved at the object
1334	  level.} The pushout-cancellation helper takes its relative tensor product over the
1335	  \emph{canonical} \(A\)-algebra structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\)
1336	  (multiplication in the left factor), whereas the carrier object produced by the
1337	  dictionaries,
1338	  the extension of scalars of \(M\) along the left inclusion \(\iota : A \to A \otimes_R R'\),
1339	  tensors over the \(A\)-module structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\) induced by \(\iota\). These
1340	  two \(A\)-module structures on
1341	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) agree on elements but are distinct as data, so the two relative
1342	  tensor products are distinct types even though their underlying abelian groups and
1343	  their \(R'\)-actions coincide. This diamond is the \emph{only} thing the helper does
1344	  not already discharge, and — because the \(R'\)-linearity is now entirely internal to
1345	  the Mathlib equivalence — it is resolved purely at the carrier level by an
1346	  identity-on-elements \(R'\)-linear bridge
1347	  \[
1348	    eT : (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\operatorname{restr}} M
1349	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1350	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\mathrm{can}} M ,
1351	  \]
1352	  the identity map on generators, which is \(R'\)-linear because both sides carry the
1353	  same \(R'\)-action (restriction of scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\)).
1354	
1355	  Composing the bridge \(eT\) with the natively \(R'\)-linear helper equivalence and
1356	  promoting to an isomorphism of module objects yields the desired bundled
1357	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism of \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\) objects. Being a composite
1358	  of linear equivalences it is an \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of its constituents
1359	  carries a flatness hypothesis.
1360	\end{proof}

1475	  attached to a morphism \(h\). Under the conjugation (mate) bijection between the composite
1476	  adjunction \((g^{*}\dashv g_{*}) . (f^{*}\dashv f_{*})\) and the adjunction
1477	  \(\bigl((f\circ g)^{*} \dashv (f\circ g)_{*}\bigr)\), the inverse pullback-composition coherence
1478	  \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{f,g})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) is carried to the pushforward-composition
1479	  coherence \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f,g})^{\mathrm{hom}}\):
1480	  \[
1481	    \operatorname{conj}\!\Bigl((g^{*}\dashv g_{*}) . (f^{*}\dashv f_{*}),\;
1482	      \bigl((f\circ g)^{*}\dashv (f\circ g)_{*}\bigr)\Bigr)
1483	      \bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{f,g})^{\mathrm{inv}}\bigr)
1484	    \;=\; (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f,g})^{\mathrm{hom}}.
1485	  \]
1486	\end{lemma}

1493	\begin{lemma}[Conjugation is an equivalence on isomorphisms]
1494	  \label{lem:conjugateIsoEquiv_mathlib}
1495	  \lean{CategoryTheory.conjugateIsoEquiv}
1496	  \mathlibok
1497	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1498	  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_1 : L_1 \dashv R_1\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_2 : L_2 \dashv R_2\) be two adjunctions
1499	  between the same pair of categories. The conjugation (mate) bijection on natural transformations
1500	  restricts to an equivalence on natural \emph{isomorphisms},
1501	  \[
1502	    \operatorname{conjIso}(\mathrm{adj}_1,\mathrm{adj}_2) :
1503	      (L_2 \cong L_1) \;\simeq\; (R_1 \cong R_2),
1504	  \]
1505	  carrying an isomorphism of left adjoints to the conjugate isomorphism of right adjoints, and back. In
1506	  particular the conjugate of an isomorphism is an isomorphism: this is the certificate that makes the
1507	  affine flat-base-change comparison invertible without inspecting its concrete sheaf assembly.
1508	\end{lemma}
1509	
1510	\begin{lemma}[The iterated mate of a left-adjoint square is the conjugate (Beck--Chevalley)]
1511	  \label{lem:iterated_mateEquiv_conjugateEquiv_mathlib}
1512	  \lean{CategoryTheory.iterated_mateEquiv_conjugateEquiv}
1513	  \mathlibok
1514	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1515	  For a square of functors whose four boundary functors are all left adjoints, with adjunctions
1516	  \(\mathrm{adj}_1, \dots, \mathrm{adj}_4\), the iterated mate (the Beck--Chevalley \(2\)-cell of the
1517	  pasted square) of a \(2\)-cell \(\alpha\) equals the conjugate of \(\alpha\) for the two composite
1518	  adjunctions:
1519	  \[
1520	    \operatorname{mate}\bigl(\operatorname{mate}(\alpha)\bigr) \;=\;
1521	      \operatorname{conj}\bigl(\mathrm{adj}_1.\mathrm{adj}_4,\ \mathrm{adj}_3.\mathrm{adj}_2\bigr)\,\alpha .
1522	  \]
1523	  Together with Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateIsoEquiv_mathlib} this is the abstract reason a Beck--Chevalley
1524	  transformation is a natural isomorphism exactly when \(\alpha\) is — the conceptual home of the
1525	  iso-ness of the affine comparison \(g^* f_*\widetilde M \to f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\), which the
1526	  project realises directly as a conjugate of the pushforward \(\Gamma\)-comparison.
1527	\end{lemma}

1529	\begin{lemma}[Composite-adjunction comparison from a right-adjoint isomorphism]
1530	  \label{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}
1531	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso}
1532	  \mathlibok
1533	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1534	  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_{01} : F_{01} \dashv G_{10}\), \(\mathrm{adj}_{12} : F_{12} \dashv G_{21}\) and
1535	  \(\mathrm{adj}_{02} : F_{02} \dashv G_{20}\) be adjunctions, and let
1536	  \(e : G_{21} \circ G_{10} \cong G_{20}\) be a natural isomorphism comparing the composite right adjoint
1537	  with \(G_{20}\). Then there is a canonical natural isomorphism of the corresponding left adjoints
1538	  \[
1539	    \operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}(e) : F_{01} \circ F_{12} \;\cong\; F_{02},
1540	  \]
1541	  namely the conjugate of \(e^{-1}\). It builds the comparison for a composite of two adjunctions out of
1542	  a single right-adjoint comparison, carrying no propositional equality data.
1543	\end{lemma}

1545	\begin{lemma}[The conjugate of the composite-comparison inverse is the right-adjoint isomorphism]
1546	  \label{lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib}
1547	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Adjunction.conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv}
1548	  \mathlibok
1549	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1550	  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}, the conjugation bijection between the
1551	  composite adjunction \(\mathrm{adj}_{01}.\mathrm{adj}_{12}\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_{02}\) sends the inverse
1552	  of the composite comparison \(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}(e)\) back to the hom of the
1553	  right-adjoint isomorphism \(e\):
1554	  \[
1555	    \operatorname{conj}\bigl(\mathrm{adj}_{01}.\mathrm{adj}_{12},\ \mathrm{adj}_{02}\bigr)
1556	      \bigl(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}(e)^{-1}\bigr) \;=\; e^{\mathrm{hom}} .
1557	  \]
1558	  This is the one-line bridge that turns a coherence among composite-adjunction comparisons into an
1559	  equation on the right-adjoint side, where the conjugate simp set closes it.
1560	\end{lemma}

1562	\begin{lemma}[Transposed unit-across-conjugate coherence]
1563	  \label{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_symm_mathlib}

1565	  \mathlibok

1630	\begin{lemma}
1631	
1632	\leanok
1633	[Pseudofunctoriality of the pullback--pushforward unit]
1634	  \label{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}
1635	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}
1636	  \uses{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib, lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib,
1637	        lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}
1638	  Let \(a : X_1 \to X_2\) and \(b : X_2 \to X_3\) be composable morphisms of schemes and let
1639	  \(N\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_{X_3}\)-module. Write \(\eta^{h}\) for the unit of the
1640	  pullback--pushforward adjunction \((h^{*}\dashv h_{*})\) attached to a morphism \(h\), and
1641	  \(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b}\), \(\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b}\) for the pseudofunctor
1642	  composition coherences. Then
1643	  \[
1644	    \eta^{b}_{N} \;\circ\; b_{*}\bigl(\eta^{a}_{\,b^{*}N}\bigr)
1645	      \;\circ\; (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{hom}}_{N}
1646	    \;=\;
1647	    \eta^{a\circ b}_{N} \;\circ\;
1648	      (a\circ b)_{*}\bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}_{N}\bigr),
1649	  \]
1650	  where composites are read left to right. In words: the unit of the composite morphism
1651	  \(a \circ b\), reindexed by the inverse pullback coherence, factors as the \(b\)-unit, followed
1652	  by \(b_{*}\) of the \(a\)-unit, followed by the pushforward coherence. This is the leg-reindex
1653	  engine consumed by Seam~2 (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}): with \(a\) an
1654	  isomorphism and \(b\) an affine \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-map it turns the generic projection-leg
1655	  unit into the affine \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-unit modulo the transparent coherences.
1656	\end{lemma}
1657	\begin{proof}
1658	  \uses{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib, lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib,
1659	        lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}
1660	  Apply the unit-across-conjugate coherence Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib} to the
1661	  composite adjunction \((b^{*}\dashv b_{*}) . (a^{*}\dashv a_{*})\), the adjunction
1662	  \(\bigl((a\circ b)^{*}\dashv (a\circ b)_{*}\bigr)\), and the left-adjoint transformation
1663	  \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}\). This yields an identity whose right-hand factor
1664	  is the conjugate (mate) of \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}\); by
1665	  Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib} that mate is exactly the pushforward
1666	  coherence \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{hom}}\). Expanding the unit of the composite
1667	  adjunction by Lemma~\ref{lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib} —
1668	  \(\bigl((b^{*}\dashv b_{*}).(a^{*}\dashv a_{*})\bigr)\text{-unit}_N
1669	    = \eta^{b}_N \circ b_{*}(\eta^{a}_{b^{*}N})\) — and reassociating the resulting composite gives
1670	  the stated equation.
1671	\end{proof}

1899	\begin{lemma}[Conjugation distributes over composition (reassoc simp lemma)]
1900	  \label{lem:conjugateEquiv_comp_mathlib}
1901	  \lean{CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv_comp}
1902	  \mathlibok

1904	  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_1 : L_1 \dashv R_1\), \(\mathrm{adj}_2 : L_2 \dashv R_2\),

2450	  \(\eta^{e}_{N} : N \to e_{*}\,e^{*}N\) of the \((e^{*} \dashv e_{*})\)-adjunction is an

2459	  is an equivalence (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}). The unit of an adjunction whose
2460	  left adjoint is an equivalence is an isomorphism; hence \(\eta^{e}_{N}\) is invertible.
2461	\end{proof}

2483	\begin{proof}
2484	  \uses{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id}

2486	  so \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) is the identity morphism; its
2487	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}\)-image is then the identity by functoriality, and its further

### Signature/description mismatches

**`lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs` (major):** The blueprint block at `lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs` carries these problems:

1. **Title and prose** say the codomain read is "assembled as the composite-adjunction comparison `leftAdjointCompIso`" of the free legs `e`, `Spec ιA`. But the pinned Lean decl `base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs` (line 1254) assembles its pullback factor as `(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp e.hom (Spec.map inclA)).symm.app`, NOT `leftAdjointCompIso`. The conj version (`base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs_conj`, line 1563) uses `conjPullbackFactor = leftAdjointCompIso …`. The blueprint description correctly describes `_conj`, not the decl it pins.

2. **`\uses` overshoot**: The block lists `lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib` and `lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib` as dependencies, but the actual pinned Lean decl `base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs` does NOT use either. These belong in the `\uses` of `lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs_conj`.

The mismatch appears to be a consequence of the iter-034/035 re-encoding: the prose and `\uses` were updated to describe the conjugate-native form, but the `\lean{...}` pin was not updated to track the newly-introduced `_conj` decl. This confuses the dependency graph.

**`gammaMap_pushforwardCongr_hom` (minor):** Blueprint states the result as "yields the identity morphism" (`= id`). The Lean gives `= eqToHom (by rw [hfg])`. Mathematically these are equal (when `hfg` is substituted, `eqToHom rfl = 𝟙 _`), but the stated type of the Lean theorem is strictly more precise than the blueprint says.
